proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.220 on venetoclax/azacitidine vs 0.166 off (n=20020 vs 29980). chi2 p=6.6e-51.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_7plus3 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.189 on 7+3 vs 0.186 off (n=22580 vs 27420). chi2 p=0.46.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_midostaurin vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.186 on midostaurin vs 0.188 off (n=7545 vs 42455). chi2 p=0.67.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_gilteritinib vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.181 on gilteritinib vs 0.189 off (n=7545 vs 42455). chi2 p=0.14.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_ivosidenib vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.188 on ivosidenib vs 0.187 off (n=3938 vs 46062). chi2 p=0.93.

analyses.code: chi2 test of treatment_enasidenib vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.186 on enasidenib vs 0.188 off (n=3972 vs 46028). chi2 p=0.79.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher objective_response rate than those without npm1_mutation.
Patients with tp53_mutation have a lower objective_response rate than those without tp53_mutation.
Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower objective_response rate than those without complex_karyotype.
Patients with flt3_itd have a different objective_response rate than those without flt3_itd.
Patients with flt3_tkd have a different objective_response rate than those without flt3_tkd.
Patients with idh1_mutation have a different objective_response rate than those without idh1_mutation.
Patients with idh2_mutation have a different objective_response rate than those without idh2_mutation.
Patients with secondary_aml have a different objective_response rate than those without secondary_aml.

analyses.code: chi2 test of npm1_mutation vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.235 in NPM1+ vs 0.167 in NPM1- (n=15014 vs 34986). chi2 p=5.8e-70.

analyses.code: chi2 test of tp53_mutation vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.166 in TP53+ vs 0.190 in TP53- (n=4985 vs 45015). chi2 p=3.5e-5.

analyses.code: chi2 test of complex_karyotype vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.168 in CK+ vs 0.191 in CK- (n=7462 vs 42538). chi2 p=3.3e-6.

analyses.code: chi2 test of flt3_itd vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.189 in FLT3-ITD+ vs 0.187 in FLT3-ITD- (n=10000 vs 40000). chi2 p=0.64.

analyses.code: chi2 test of flt3_tkd vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.180 in FLT3-TKD+ vs 0.188 in FLT3-TKD- (n=2815 vs 47185). chi2 p=0.34.

analyses.code: chi2 test of idh1_mutation vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.191 in IDH1+ vs 0.187 in IDH1- (n=3624 vs 46376). chi2 p=0.60.

analyses.code: chi2 test of idh2_mutation vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.192 in IDH2+ vs 0.187 in IDH2- (n=4679 vs 45321). chi2 p=0.47.

analyses.code: chi2 test of secondary_aml vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.188 in sec-AML vs 0.187 in de novo (n=12301 vs 37699). chi2 p=0.85.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than males.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a lower objective_response than fit patients.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ecog_ps coef=-0.353 per unit, p=1.6e-90. Higher ECOG -> lower response.
age coef=-0.0002 per year, p=0.88. No detectable main effect of age on response.

analyses.code: chi2 test of sex_female vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.189 female vs 0.186 male, p=0.42.

analyses.code: chi2 test of unfit_for_intensive vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.221 unfit vs 0.165 fit (n=20056 vs 29944). p=1.9e-55. SURPRISING: unfit patients have HIGHER response unconditionally.
blast coef=-0.0049 per percent, p=2.4e-16. Higher marrow blasts -> lower response.
coef=-0.0357 per percent, p=1.1e-30. More weight loss -> lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: albumin coef=+0.117 per g/dL, p=2.9e-7. Higher albumin -> higher response.
CRP coef=-0.0064 per mg/L, p=6.1e-6. Higher CRP -> lower response.
WBC coef=-0.00177 per K/uL, p=1.9e-4. Higher WBC -> lower response.
LDH coef=-0.00013 per U/L, p=0.35. No significant main effect.
NLR coef=-0.00055 per unit, p=0.92. No significant main effect.
Hgb coef=-0.0092 per g/dL, p=0.15. No significant main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response more in flt3_itd-positive patients than in flt3_itd-negative patients (positive interaction).
treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response more in flt3_tkd-positive patients than in flt3_tkd-negative patients (positive interaction).
treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response more in flt3_itd-positive patients than in flt3_itd-negative patients (positive interaction).
treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response more in flt3_tkd-positive patients than in flt3_tkd-negative patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates flat: ITD+/tx+ 0.176 vs ITD+/tx- 0.191; ITD-/tx+ 0.188 vs ITD-/tx- 0.187. Interaction coef=-0.106, p=0.19.
TKD+/tx+ 0.176 vs TKD+/tx- 0.181; TKD-/tx+ 0.186 vs TKD-/tx- 0.188. Interaction coef=-0.023, p=0.87.
ITD+/tx+ 0.191 vs ITD+/tx- 0.189; ITD-/tx+ 0.179 vs ITD-/tx- 0.189. Interaction coef=+0.079, p=0.32.
TKD+/tx+ 0.151 vs TKD+/tx- 0.186; TKD-/tx+ 0.183 vs TKD-/tx- 0.189. Interaction coef=-0.213, p=0.16.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response more in idh1_mutation-positive patients than in idh1_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction).
treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response more in idh2_mutation-positive patients than in idh2_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: IDH1+/tx+ 0.154 vs IDH1+/tx- 0.194; IDH1-/tx+ 0.191 vs IDH1-/tx- 0.187. Interaction coef=-0.304, p=0.077 (wrong direction, NS).
IDH2+/tx+ 0.195 vs IDH2+/tx- 0.191; IDH2-/tx+ 0.185 vs IDH2-/tx- 0.187. Interaction coef=+0.039, p=0.79.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more in unfit_for_intensive patients than in fit patients (positive interaction).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more in npm1_mutation-positive patients than in npm1-negative patients (positive interaction).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has reduced efficacy in tp53_mutation-positive patients (negative interaction).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has reduced efficacy in complex_karyotype-positive patients (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Unfit+/tx+ 0.308 vs unfit+/tx- 0.163 (RD +0.145); fit/tx+ 0.161 vs fit/tx- 0.168 (RD -0.008). Interaction coef=+0.883, p=1.1e-78.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a larger benefit on objective_response in patients who are not unfit_for_intensive (negative interaction with unfit).
treatment_7plus3 efficacy on objective_response is reduced by complex_karyotype (negative interaction).
treatment_7plus3 efficacy decreases with older age_years (negative interaction).
treatment_7plus3 efficacy decreases with higher ecog_ps (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Unfit+/tx+ 0.221 vs unfit+/tx- 0.221; fit/tx+ 0.167 vs fit/tx- 0.163. Interaction coef=-0.027, p=0.56. No heterogeneity.
CK+/tx+ 0.156 vs CK+/tx- 0.179; CK-/tx+ 0.195 vs CK-/tx- 0.188. Interaction coef=-0.213, p=1.5e-3. 7+3 underperforms in CK+.
Interaction coef=+0.0012, p=0.56. No age modulation.
Interaction coef=-0.015, p=0.66. No ECOG modulation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features and treatments, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains independently associated with higher objective_response.

After adjustment, ecog_ps, npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, crp_mg_l, and wbc_k_per_ul each remain independently predictive of objective_response.
The other five treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) have no independent main effect on objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit with all features + treatments
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA adjusted coef=+0.362, p=8.2e-54. Independent positive main effect.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit with all features + treatments
analyses.result_summary: All listed predictors retain p<0.05: ecog -0.36 (p=9e-93), npm1 +0.44 (p=2e-71), unfit +0.37 (p=2e-57), wt_loss -0.037 (p=3e-31), blast -0.0052 (p=1e-17), albumin +0.12 (p=1e-7), complex_karyotype -0.17 (p=6e-7), tp53 -0.19 (p=4e-6), crp -0.0065 (p=6e-6), wbc -0.0018 (p=1e-4).
Adjusted main coefficients: midostaurin -0.009 (p=0.79), gilteritinib -0.050 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.019 (p=0.65), enasidenib -0.011 (p=0.80), 7+3 +0.014 (p=0.55). None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, the strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation.
Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, the second-strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive.
Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a significantly negative interaction with tp53_mutation and with complex_karyotype.

analyses.code: Systematic 6 treatments x 20 features interaction screen via logit
analyses.result_summary: Top hit: VEN/AZA x NPM1, interaction coef=+1.055, p=5.5e-102 (smallest p of 120 pairs).

analyses.code: Systematic 6 treatments x 20 features interaction screen via logit
analyses.result_summary: Second-strongest: VEN/AZA x unfit_for_intensive, interaction coef=+0.883, p=1.1e-78.

analyses.code: Systematic 6 treatments x 20 features interaction screen via logit
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA x TP53 coef=-0.409, p=4.7e-7; VEN/AZA x CK coef=-0.306, p=6.0e-6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is concentrated specifically in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation-positive AND unfit_for_intensive (three-way interaction).
Within the npm1_mutation-positive AND unfit_for_intensive subgroup, the venetoclax/azacitidine response rate is dramatically higher than control (>50 percentage point absolute increase).
Outside the joint NPM1+/unfit subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: logit y ~ tx + npm1 + unfit + npm1*unfit + tx*npm1 + tx*unfit + tx*npm1*unfit
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction tx*npm1*unfit coef=+2.13, p=1.6e-91. Strong positive triple interaction.
rate_tx=0.633 (n=2401) vs rate_ctrl=0.160 (n=3585), risk diff +0.474, chi-sq p<<1e-300.
Within NPM1- only OR fit only: rates ~0.16-0.17 in both arms, RD ranging from -0.010 to +0.005, all p>0.07.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup, tp53_mutation abolishes the venetoclax/azacitidine treatment effect (negative tx*tp53 interaction).
Within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup, complex_karyotype abolishes the venetoclax/azacitidine treatment effect (negative tx*complex_karyotype interaction).

The complete subgroup defining venetoclax/azacitidine benefit is npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in this group response rate on treatment >75% vs <20% off.

analyses.code: Within npm1=1 & unfit=1 subset: logit y ~ tx + tp53 + tx*tp53
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+/unfit (n=5986): TP53-/tx 0.691 vs TP53-/ctrl 0.157; TP53+/tx 0.188 vs TP53+/ctrl 0.183. tx*tp53 interaction coef=-2.45, p=1.1e-29.

analyses.code: Within npm1=1 & unfit=1 subset: logit y ~ tx + ck + tx*ck
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+/unfit: CK-/tx 0.717 vs CK-/ctrl 0.161; CK+/tx 0.184 vs CK+/ctrl 0.152. tx*ck interaction coef=-2.35, p=3.0e-34.

analyses.code: Stratified rates in npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enasidenib has a sex-specific effect on objective_response: females (sex_female=1) benefit, males (sex_female=0) do not (positive tx*sex interaction).
treatment_enasidenib has greater benefit at lower hemoglobin_g_dl (negative tx*hemoglobin interaction).

analyses.code: Adjusted logit y ~ covariates + enasidenib + sex_female + tx*sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tx*sex_female interaction coef=+0.320, p=2.2e-4. Stratified rates: female/tx 0.212 vs female/ctrl 0.187 (RD +0.025); male/tx 0.165 vs male/ctrl 0.188 (RD -0.023).

analyses.code: Adjusted logit y ~ covariates + enasidenib + hgb + tx*hgb
analyses.result_summary: tx*hemoglobin interaction coef=-0.081 per g/dL, p=9.2e-4 in adjusted model. Effect attenuates at higher hgb.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the five non-VEN/AZA treatments, no concentrated treatment-benefit subgroup with a risk difference >0.20 and adequate sample size exists.
treatment_7plus3 worsens objective_response in patients with complex_karyotype (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Pairwise binary feature subgroup search across all five non-VEN treatments
analyses.result_summary: No subgroup with RD>0.20 found at n>=50/arm for midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, or 7+3. Largest signal in non-VEN group is enasidenib RD ~+0.05 in NPM1+/female.
tx*ck interaction coef=-0.225, p=9.8e-4 in adjusted model. Within CK+: 7+3 rate 0.156 vs ctrl 0.179 (RD -0.023).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment (including all six treatments and patient features), the triple interaction tx_VEN/AZA * npm1_mutation * unfit_for_intensive remains the dominant effect.

After full multivariable adjustment, both tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype each have significantly negative interactions with tx_VEN/AZA, indicating each independently suppresses the venetoclax effect.

analyses.code: Logit with all features + 6 treatments + tx*npm1, tx*unfit, tx*tp53, tx*ck, tx*npm1*unfit
analyses.result_summary: tx*npm1*unfit coef=+2.27, p=7.7e-182 (dominant single effect after full adjustment).
tx*tp53 coef=-0.563, p=6.4e-11; tx*ck coef=-0.392, p=4.0e-8. Each remains significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive search over all 3-feature binary subgroups for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine identifies the npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND complex_karyotype=0 (or equivalently AND tp53=0) subgroup as the highest-RD subgroup.

An exhaustive search over all 4-feature binary subgroups for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine identifies npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 as the maximal-RD definition.

analyses.code: Brute-force 3-of-10 binary feature subgroup enumeration with tx vs ctrl rates
analyses.result_summary: Top 3-feature subgroup by RD: unfit=1 & ck=0 & npm1=1: rate_tx=0.717 (n=2025) vs rate_ctrl=0.161 (n=3052), RD=+0.556. Second: unfit=1 & npm1=1 & tp53=0: RD=+0.534.
Best 4-feature subgroup: unfit=1 & ck=0 & npm1=1 & tp53=0: rate_tx=0.786 (n=1790) vs rate_ctrl=0.158 (n=2741), RD=+0.628. No other 4-feature subgroup exceeds this RD with adequate size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_midostaurin reveals no subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive risk difference (RD>0.10) at adequate sample size.
An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_gilteritinib reveals no subgroup with RD>0.10 at adequate sample size.
An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_ivosidenib reveals no subgroup with RD>0.10 at adequate sample size.
An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_enasidenib reveals only modest subgroup signals; the largest identifies sex_female=1 & idh1_mutation=1 & npm1_mutation=0 with RD ~+0.09.
treatment_7plus3 shows a notable negative subgroup at complex_karyotype=1 & flt3_itd=1 & npm1_mutation=1: 7+3 reduces objective_response by ~12 percentage points.

analyses.result_summary: Top midostaurin RD subgroup is sex_female=1 & ck=1 & tp53=1 with RD=+0.103, n_tx=56. No subgroup with RD>0.10 and adequate size.
Top positive gilteritinib subgroup (CK=0 & FLT3-ITD=1 & IDH1=1) RD=+0.093, n_tx=96, p=0.049. No clinically meaningful enrichment.
Top ivosidenib positive: sex_female=1 & flt3_tkd=1 & npm1=0 RD=+0.116, n_tx=63. Bottom: secondary_aml=1 & ck=0 & idh2=1 RD=-0.150 (likely chance). No reliable benefit.
Top enasidenib subgroups all involve sex_female=1 with NPM1+ or IDH1/IDH2: e.g. sex_female=1 & unfit=1 & idh2=1 RD=+0.118, n_tx=61, p=0.06. Modest sex-driven signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0 subgroup, after adjustment for ECOG, age, sex, mutations, labs, and other treatments, the venetoclax/azacitidine effect remains very large (OR>10).
Outside the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0 subgroup, after similar adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.
NPM1+ alone (without unfit) does not produce a venetoclax/azacitidine response benefit.
Unfit alone (without NPM1) does not produce a venetoclax/azacitidine response benefit.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit on subgroup: y ~ all covariates + tx
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup (n=4531): adjusted VEN/AZA coef=+3.07, OR~21.6, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit on complement: y ~ all covariates + tx
analyses.result_summary: Outside subgroup (n=45469): adjusted VEN/AZA coef=-0.017, p=0.53.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+/unfit/no-CK/no-TP53 patients, restricting to those without treatment_7plus3 (i.e., monotherapy ven/aza) yields an even higher response.
Among NPM1+/unfit/no-CK/no-TP53 patients on venetoclax/azacitidine, the response rate is uniformly high regardless of FLT3 status, IDH status, sex, and age.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup-level response on VEN/AZA approximates 0.78-0.79 across substrata (sex, FLT3-ITD, IDH, secondary AML); response off VEN/AZA approximates 0.15-0.16 in same substrata. Subgroup definition is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final overarching subgroup hypothesis for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: objective_response is markedly higher on treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine specifically among patients who satisfy ALL of: npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0; outside this conjunction the treatment has no effect.

Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_7plus3: objective_response is reduced on treatment_7plus3 vs no treatment_7plus3 specifically among patients with complex_karyotype=1 (especially in combination with flt3_itd=1 and/or npm1_mutation=1); outside complex_karyotype, 7+3 has no significant effect.

Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_enasidenib: objective_response is modestly higher on treatment_enasidenib vs not specifically among female patients (sex_female=1), with the strongest signal in unfit/IDH-mutated females; in males, enasidenib has no benefit (and may slightly harm).
Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_midostaurin: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_midostaurin provides a meaningful objective_response benefit.
Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_gilteritinib: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_gilteritinib provides a meaningful objective_response benefit.

Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_ivosidenib: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_ivosidenib provides a meaningful objective_response benefit; in particular, idh1_mutation-positive patients do NOT benefit.

analyses.code: Final stratified analysis: tx effect inside vs outside NPM1+/unfit/no-TP53/no-CK

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tx*ck coef=-0.225, p=9.8e-4. Within CK+: 7+3 rate 0.156 vs no-7+3 0.179, RD=-0.023. Within CK-: 7+3 rate 0.195 vs 0.188, RD=+0.007.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit y ~ covariates + enasidenib + sex_female + tx*sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tx*sex_female coef=+0.320, p=2.2e-4. Stratified rates: female on tx 0.212 vs off 0.187 (RD +0.025); male on tx 0.165 vs off 0.188 (RD -0.023).

analyses.code: Best subgroup search results for midostaurin
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main coef=-0.009 (p=0.79). Best subgroup RD=+0.103 with n=56, not significant after multiplicity. No reliable beneficial subgroup.

analyses.code: Best subgroup search results for gilteritinib
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main coef=-0.050 (p=0.13). Best subgroup RD=+0.093 with n=96, p=0.049, not robust. No reliable beneficial subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified ivosidenib in IDH1+ vs IDH1-
analyses.result_summary: IDH1+/tx rate 0.154 (n=298) vs IDH1+/ctrl 0.194 (n=3326): RD=-0.040 (p=0.075). Adjusted main coef=+0.019, p=0.65. No benefit; if anything trend toward harm in IDH1+.
